history.content: Can you go ahead and test out the new tool system, the categories, etc - do they all work well?

Yep — I tested it live.

### What worked
- ✅ `request_tool_category('browser')`
- ✅ `request_tool_category('desktop')`
- ✅ `request_tool_category('team_ops')`
- ✅ `request_tool_category('scheduling')`
- ✅ `request_tool_category('source_write')`
- ✅ `request_tool_category('integrations')`

All six categories activated successfully in-session.

### What looks off
- I also ran one harmless command test (`run_command`) and it returned a command-parser restriction error for `echo`, which suggests `run_command` in this environment is currently allowlisted (not full shell passthrough).

### Overall verdict
- **Category activation system itself is healthy.**
- **At least one non-category tool (`run_command`) has environment-level command restrictions**, so “everything works” depends on whether that restriction is intentional.

If you want, I can do a deeper pass next: one real functional call per category toolset ([REDACTED-HE]) and give you a precise pass/fail matrix.
